# Implementation Plan: Spec Kit Automated Workflow Orchestration

**Branch**: `008-speckit-auto-workflow` | **Date**: 2026-05-18 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)

**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/008-speckit-auto-workflow/spec.md`

**Note**: This file was generated by `/speckit-plan`.

## Summary

Deliver an end-to-end Spec Kit pipeline orchestrator for Claude Code that: (a) accepts a feature description as a single command invocation, (b) runs all Spec Kit stages inline without pausing for user re-invocation between hooks, (c) enforces safety guards before auto-implementation, and (d) produces a structured post-implementation review. The orchestrator is implemented as a Claude Code skill (`speckit-auto`) that invokes 8 dedicated sub-agent definitions via the Agent tool. A standalone Bash guard script detects safety keywords and CRITICAL analyze output, and pipeline state is persisted to `pipeline-state.json` for cross-session resume.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Markdown (agent/skill definitions), Bash (guard script, pipeline state), JSON (state file, settings.json hooks)

**Primary Dependencies**: Claude Code CLI (sub-agent / skill mechanism); Spec Kit skills (speckit-specify, speckit-clarify, speckit-plan, speckit-tasks, speckit-analyze, speckit-implement already installed)

**Storage**: File system — `pipeline-state.json` under the feature directory; all stage outputs under `specs/<NNN>-<name>/`

**Testing**: Guard script unit tests via Bash (`bash -c "..."`); acceptance scenario validation via `speckit-reviewer` per FR-009

**Target Platform**: Developer laptop (macOS/Linux), Claude Code CLI

**Project Type**: Developer tooling — Claude Code configuration files + Bash scripts

**Performance Goals**: Inline hook execution eliminates re-invocation round-trips between stages. No quantitative LLM inference targets (dominated by external latency). Guard script execution ≤ 100ms per invocation.

**Constraints**: Guard script must run in Bash (FR-012). All stage outputs before implement must be confined to `specs/`, `.claude/`, `.specify/` paths (FR-007).

**Scale/Scope**: Single-developer tool; no concurrency requirements.

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-checked after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| I. Spec Before Code | ✅ Pass | spec.md complete with clarifications |
| II. Single Source of Truth | ✅ Pass | Safety keyword list defined once in `speckit-guards/SKILL.md`; pipeline-state schema defined once in `contracts/pipeline-state-schema.md` |
| III. Explicit Overrides Only | ✅ Pass | N/A — no deviations from spec required |
| IV. Version-Pinned Stack | ✅ Pass | No new npm deps; existing `@types/bun` 1.3.14 and `typescript` 6.0.3 are current |
| V. Type Safety | ⚠️ Justified Deviation | Guard script is Bash (required by FR-012 — developers need a script they can run without Claude Code). No runtime typing in Bash. Mitigated: guard script output is strictly formatted JSON consumed via `jq` or parsed inline. |
| VI. Code Quality | ✅ Pass | Guard script ≤ 100 lines; each agent definition is single-responsibility |
| VII. Testing Standards | ✅ Pass | Guard script must have test cases covering all 6 safety keyword variants and CRITICAL pattern detection. Acceptance scenarios in spec.md are the test spec for agent outputs. |
| VIII–X. UX/Design/a11y | N/A | No UI surface |
| XI. Performance Budgets | ⚠️ Justified Deviation | No user-facing surface; performance dominated by LLM inference latency outside this feature's control. Guard script ≤ 100ms budget declared for the scripted portion. |
| XII. Security and Privacy | ✅ Pass | Guard script reads feature descriptions; no secrets; no PII risk. Feature descriptions should not contain credentials (existing project guidance). |
| XIII. Module Boundaries | ✅ Pass | Each agent file self-contained; skills self-contained; guard script standalone |
| XIV. Local-First | ✅ Pass | All deliverables are markdown/Bash/JSON files; no cloud dependency |
| XV. AI Agent Governance | ✅ Pass | This feature IS the governance implementation; agents consult spec+plan+tasks before acting |
| XVI. Definition of Done | ✅ Pass | Each task will map to a spec requirement; guard script tests are required for task completion |

**Justified Deviations** (Principle III documentation):

| Violation | Why Needed | Simpler Alternative Rejected Because |
|-----------|------------|-------------------------------------|
| Principle V — Bash has no type safety | FR-012 explicitly requires a standalone Bash guard script developers can run without Claude Code | TypeScript/Bun alternative would add a runtime dependency and defeat the "standalone" requirement |
| Principle XI — No perf budget for orchestration | LLM inference latency dominates; orchestration overhead is negligible | Synthetic benchmarks would produce meaningless numbers and create false precision |

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/008-speckit-auto-workflow/
├── plan.md                          # This file
├── research.md                      # Phase 0 research findings
├── data-model.md                    # Entity definitions and state machine
├── contracts/
│   ├── pipeline-state-schema.md     # pipeline-state.json contract
│   ├── guard-result-schema.md       # guard.sh stdout contract
│   └── agent-interface.md           # Sub-agent input/output contracts
├── checklists/
│   └── requirements.md
├── version-guard-report.md
└── tasks.md                         # Created by /speckit-tasks
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
.claude/
├── agents/
│   ├── speckit-specifier.md         # Stage 1: run /speckit-specify
│   ├── speckit-clarifier.md         # Stage 2: run /speckit-clarify
│   ├── speckit-checklister.md       # Stage 3: run /speckit-checklist
│   ├── speckit-planner.md           # Stage 4: run /speckit-plan
│   ├── speckit-tasker.md            # Stage 5: run /speckit-tasks
│   ├── speckit-analyzer.md          # Stage 6: run /speckit-analyze; emit SEVERITY: CRITICAL
│   ├── speckit-implementer.md       # Stage 7: run /speckit-implement
│   └── speckit-reviewer.md          # Stage 8: validate acceptance scenarios + diff + tasks
├── skills/
│   ├── speckit-auto/
│   │   └── SKILL.md                 # Orchestrator: reads mode, invokes agents, writes pipeline-state.json
│   ├── speckit-rules/
│   │   └── SKILL.md                 # Pipeline stage sequencing rules (referenced by orchestrator)
│   └── speckit-guards/
│       ├── SKILL.md                 # Safety enforcement logic + keyword list
│       └── scripts/
│           └── guard.sh             # Standalone Bash guard: keyword + CRITICAL detection → JSON
└── settings.json                    # Hook definitions for speckit-auto stages
```

**Structure Decision**: Developer-tooling layout. All deliverables are configuration/script files under `.claude/`. No `src/` or `tests/` directory is created; guard script tests live as inline test cases in `guard.sh` (invoked with `--test` flag) to keep the script self-contained and standalone.

## Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations beyond the two justified deviations documented above.
